title: CNN This Morning · 2026-06-15 · 美伊霍尔木兹海峡协议
summary: 美国与伊朗达成协议，将于周五开放霍尔木兹海峡，但伊朗要求美国先解除封锁、停止军事行动并解冻资金，美方否认立即放款。前总统奥巴马质疑协议效果。以色列不满协议未涉及核问题及代理人武装。此外，一架跳伞飞机坠毁全员遇难，参议员麦康奈尔住院，英国拟推社交媒体禁令，UFC在白宫草坪举办比赛，卡罗来纳飓风队夺得斯坦利杯。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: Today in the group chat, both the U.S. and Iran say there's an agreement to open the Strait of Hormuz on Friday. Why not open it right away?
paragraphs.cn: 今天在群聊中，美国和伊朗都表示已达成协议，将于周五开放霍尔木兹海峡。为什么不立即开放呢？

paragraphs.speaker: Mike Waltz
paragraphs.en: The Iranians are incredibly difficult negotiators.
paragraphs.cn: 伊朗人是极其难缠的谈判对手。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And former President Obama says he's doubtful Trump's agreement will be much different than his. True or just sour grapes?
paragraphs.cn: 前总统奥巴马表示，他怀疑特朗普的协议会与他的协议有很大不同。这是事实还是酸葡萄心理？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Just getting to enjoy God's green Earth and then have something like this turn so tragic.
paragraphs.cn: 刚刚还在享受上帝创造的绿色地球，然后发生这样的事，变得如此悲惨。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: A plane full of skydivers crashes right after takeoff, killing everyone on board. We're going to talk about what happened moments before it went down. And Senator Mitch McConnell hospitalized. What we know about his condition this morning.
paragraphs.cn: 一架满载跳伞者的飞机起飞后不久坠毁，机上人员全部遇难。我们将讨论飞机坠毁前发生了什么。参议院少数党领袖米奇·麦康奈尔住院。我们了解到的他今早的状况。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Too much time spent on screens is not good for anyone. Never mind someone's brain is still developing.
paragraphs.cn: 长时间盯着屏幕对任何人都没有好处。更不用说有些人的大脑还在发育中。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: In the U.K., they want to give their kids their childhood back. But will a new social media ban actually work?
paragraphs.cn: 在英国，他们希望把童年还给孩子。但新的社交媒体禁令真的能奏效吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Our country was built on fighting against -- you know, for independence. UFC fight night at the White House lawn. What could the event's legacy be? And the Carolina Hurricanes are Stanley Cup champions, capping off the season with a shutout over the Las Vegas Golden Knights. A diplomatic breakthrough: The U.S. and Iran say they have reached a deal to end the war and to reopen the strait of Hormuz. It will take effect on Friday. We're going to talk about why they're waiting. J.D. VANCE, U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 我们的国家是建立在反抗 -- -- 你知道，争取独立的基础上的。白宫草坪上的UFC格斗之夜。这一赛事会留下什么遗产？卡罗莱纳飓风队以零封拉斯维加斯金骑士队夺得斯坦利杯，为本赛季画上句号。一项外交突破：美国和伊朗表示已达成结束战争并重新开放霍尔木兹海峡的协议。该协议将于周五生效。我们将讨论他们为何要等待。美国副总统J.D.万斯（电话连线）：

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: VICE PRESIDENT (via phone): Sometimes these ceasefires take a little bit of time to take root. People are very good over there at fighting each other. I'm not going to say that everybody is going to sing kumbaya tomorrow. It's going to take a little bit of time to learn the ways of peace. But I do think we took a major, major step.
paragraphs.cn: 副总统（电话连线）：有时这些停火需要一点时间才能扎根。那里的人们非常擅长互相争斗。我不会说明天大家就会一起唱《Kumbaya》。学习和平之道需要一点时间。但我确实认为我们迈出了非常重大的一步。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish. We're going to begin with the commitment from the U.S. and Iran to end the months- long conflict. But already, there's a bit of a snag. Tehran saying that 60 days of nuclear negotiations will begin once the U.S. meets three commitments. They want the U.S. to end the naval blockade; end military operations against Iran; and release billions of dollars in frozen Iranian funds.
paragraphs.cn: 大家早上好。我是奥黛·科尼什。我们首先从美国和伊朗承诺结束长达数月的冲突开始。但已经出现了一点小问题。德黑兰表示，一旦美国满足三项承诺，为期60天的核谈判将开始。他们要求美国结束海上封锁；停止针对伊朗的军事行动；并解冻数十亿美元的伊朗资金。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: KAZEM GHARIBABADI, IRAN DEPUTY FOREIGN MINISTER (through translator): This memorandum of understanding was reached, despite our complete distrust of our enemies. We have no trust in the enemy. Our trust lies in our military strength, our diplomatic capabilities, and the support and unity of our own people. That is why the implementation of this memorandum of understanding is extremely important. We have specific plans in place to monitor the implementation of U.S. commitments under this memorandum.
paragraphs.cn: 伊朗副外长卡泽姆·加里巴巴迪（通过翻译）：尽管我们完全不信任敌人，这份谅解备忘录还是达成了。我们不信任敌人。我们信任的是我们的军事实力、外交能力以及人民的支持与团结。这就是为什么执行这份谅解备忘录极其重要。我们有具体计划来监督美国履行该备忘录下的承诺。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But a U.S. official is saying that no funds will be released before Iran implements its commitments. So, could this derail things? I'm going to bring in CNN global affairs commentator and former deputy Pentagon press secretary, Sabrina Singh. So, Sabrina, good morning. Thank you for being here.
paragraphs.cn: 但一位美国官员表示，在伊朗履行其承诺之前，不会解冻任何资金。那么，这会不会破坏事情进展？下面有请CNN全球事务评论员、前五角大楼副新闻秘书萨布丽娜·辛格。萨布丽娜，早上好。感谢你的到来。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, first, I want to just talk about the latest Iran saying, Look, we're not doing anything until money is unfrozen. And then the U.S. disputing that. Here's the U.S. ambassador to the U.N., Mike Waltz, speaking on Sunday. We'll talk on the other side of that.
paragraphs.cn: 首先，我想谈谈伊朗的最新表态：他们说，在资金解冻之前，我们什么也不做。而美国对此提出异议。这是美国驻联合国大使迈克·沃尔茨周日的发言。我们稍后再讨论。

paragraphs.speaker: Waltz
paragraphs.en: The Iranians are incredibly difficult negotiators, coupled -- coupled with the fact that they're having a very hard time getting guidance from their supreme leader. And they're not always on the same page within their team, between the civilians and military. So, you know, the team has had their hands full dealing with them. But the president has every intent to get this done.
paragraphs.cn: 伊朗人是极其难缠的谈判对手，再加上他们很难从最高领袖那里获得指示。而且他们团队内部，文职和军方之间意见并不总是一致。所以，你知道，美方团队与他们打交道已经忙得不可开交。但总统完全有意愿完成这件事。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: He sort of nodded to something, I think, that maybe people don't fully appreciate, which is that Iran's regime is pretty much in place, even as though a tier of leadership was killed in these strikes. So, answer the question that he's raising here of, like, negotiation, the money. What are the complications?
paragraphs.cn: 他似乎在暗示某件事，我想可能人们没有完全意识到，那就是伊朗政权基本上仍然稳固，尽管在这些打击中有一层领导层被消灭了。那么，回答他提出的问题：谈判、资金。复杂之处在哪里？

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: The complications here is that Iran is very skilled at negotiating. And this regime, in particular, is more hardline than the previous regime. I mean, they are willing to incur the costs and the military strikes that the U.S. and Israel have conducted over, we've seen, a significant amount of time. I think what's interesting about this memorandum of understanding, or this new agreement, is it's only there to open the straits.
paragraphs.cn: 复杂之处在于伊朗非常擅长谈判。而且这个政权尤其比之前的政权更强硬。我的意思是，他们愿意承受美国和以色列长期实施的军事打击代价。我认为这份谅解备忘录或新协议的有趣之处在于，它只是为了开放海峡。

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: It does not address the thorniest issues, which we know Iran is really going to drag its feet on, which are the nuclear -- its nuclear program and the highly enriched uranium that is still sitting in Iran. And so, while this agreement basically tries to restore the status to pre-war status, it still doesn't address those nuclear agreements. And Iran, like -- like Mike Waltz was saying, Iran knows how to play the game to draw the United States out.
paragraphs.cn: 它没有解决最棘手的问题，我们知道伊朗会在这方面拖延，那就是其核计划以及仍存放在伊朗的高浓缩铀。因此，尽管这项协议试图将局势恢复到战前状态，但它仍然没有解决那些核协议。而伊朗，就像迈克·沃尔茨所说，知道如何玩弄游戏来拖住美国。

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: If we're talking about negotiating, at minimum, within 60 days, that is bringing us up right up to the midterms. And I think Iran knows that the United States is not going to want to start up kinetic action right before the midterms, right before voting starts in (UNINTELLIGIBLE) --
paragraphs.cn: 如果我们谈论谈判，至少60天内，那正好赶上中期选举。我认为伊朗知道美国不会想在中期选举前、投票开始前启动军事行动。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Two military questions. One, when we say the strait is opening, does that mean that they're going to -- Iran just backs down, the U.S. backs down? Are there mines in the strait? Like, are there still dangers there?
paragraphs.cn: 两个军事问题。第一，当我们说海峡开放，是否意味着伊朗让步、美国让步？海峡里有水雷吗？那里仍然有危险吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: I think both sides could say today that the strait is open and that they're clearing the mines from the strait. But the only ones that are going to determine if the strait is open are the shipping companies. Are they willing to allow their ships to start going through the strait and potentially incurring costs if, let's say, a drone is launched from Iran or there's crossfire between the two sides? So, even though Donald Trump is saying on Friday the strait is open, it is still --
paragraphs.cn: 我认为双方今天都可以说海峡是开放的，并且正在清除海峡中的水雷。但唯一能决定海峡是否开放的是航运公司。他们是否愿意让船只开始通过海峡，并可能承担成本，比如伊朗发射无人机或双方交火？所以，尽管唐纳德·特朗普周五说海峡开放了，但它仍然 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: We won't know until ships pass through.
paragraphs.cn: 我们要等船只通过后才能知道。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Last thing militarily, Israel.
paragraphs.cn: 最后是军事方面，以色列。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Israel, we're going to talk about in this program, across the political spectrum, unhappy with this deal. And in part because of military reasons. They say it doesn't address their issues around proxies that Iran backs like Hezbollah. It doesn't even talk about ballistic missiles. That's not even in the conversation around the memorandum of understanding, at least so far. This is kind of the same criticism that the Obama deal had.
paragraphs.cn: 以色列，我们将在本期节目中讨论，该国各政治派别都对这项协议感到不满。部分原因是出于军事考虑。他们表示，该协议没有解决伊朗支持的代理人（如真主党）问题。甚至没有提及弹道导弹。至少在目前，谅解备忘录的讨论中甚至没有涉及这一点。这与奥巴马协议受到的批评如出一辙。

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: I mean, you might not have agreed with the JCPOA, but that was something -- under the Obama administration. But that was something that took almost two years to negotiate. Now, with Israel, what's interesting about this agreement that this administration has negotiated, Israel is not party to the agreement. And they have said -- I mean, I think as early as this morning, that they might continue to conduct operations in Lebanon. So, between now and Friday, things could get derailed very quickly.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，你可能不同意《联合全面行动计划》，但那是奥巴马政府时期达成的协议，花了近两年时间谈判。而现在，关于本届政府谈判的这项协议，有趣的是以色列并非协议方。他们表示 -- -- 我认为最早在今天早上 -- -- 他们可能会继续在黎巴嫩开展行动。因此，从现在到周五，局势可能很快失控。

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: I think President Trump, he wanted a win on his birthday, which he -- which he got this agreement. And he also wanted to walk into the G-7 with saying, Hey, this war is coming to an end. European partners, now it's time to do your part and help us carry on the strait (ph).
paragraphs.cn: 我认为特朗普总统想在生日当天取得一场胜利，他确实得到了这份协议。他还想带着"嘿，这场战争即将结束"的姿态走进七国集团峰会。欧洲伙伴们，现在是时候尽你们的一份力，帮助我们继续维持海峡局势了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Can we do that if Israel has seized an amount of land that's, like, around the size of New York City.
paragraphs.cn: 如果以色列占领了大约相当于纽约市大小的土地，我们还能做到吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And says it's going to continue its -- its battle against Hezbollah?
paragraphs.cn: 并且表示将继续打击真主党？

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: I think it's going to be very difficult if Israel continues to conduct military operations for -- for Iran to agree to terms with the United States when it's going to say to the United States, this is also an aggressor in this war that also struck our country. You need to pull them back. And that's why you saw the president sort of react in some of these interviews that he was giving on Sunday, saying, you know, what is Bibi doing? Because he does not want to see this agreement go off the rails.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为，如果以色列继续开展军事行动，伊朗很难与美国达成协议，因为伊朗会对美国说，你们也是这场战争中的侵略者，也袭击了我们的国家。你们需要让他们撤出。这就是为什么你会看到总统在周日的一些采访中做出反应，说，你知道，比比在做什么？因为他不想看到这项协议脱轨。

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: OK, Sabrina, thank you so much for giving us some of the update there. Coming up on CNN THIS MORNING, President Trump, as Sabrina just said, has been calling out Benjamin Netanyahu. So, we're going to talk about why Trump would be saying that Netanyahu has, quote, "no 'F'-ing judgment' after the Israeli P.M. hit Lebanon right after the announcement of an agreement with Iran. Plus, one of the deadliest U.S. skydiving plane crashes in decades.
paragraphs.cn: 好的，萨布丽娜，非常感谢你为我们带来最新消息。接下来在《CNN今晨》节目中，正如萨布丽娜所说，特朗普总统一直在点名批评本雅明·内塔尼亚胡。因此，我们将讨论为什么特朗普会说内塔尼亚胡"毫无判断力"，因为以色列总理在与伊朗达成协议宣布后立即打击了黎巴嫩。此外，还有美国数十年来最致命的跳伞飞机坠毁事故之一。

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: We're going to talk about what one witness had to say right before the plane went down. And will you see ICE at the polling stations for the midterms? The homeland security secretary is not ruling it out.
paragraphs.cn: 我们将讨论一位目击者在飞机坠毁前所说的话。另外，中期选举期间你会在投票站看到移民和海关执法局人员吗？国土安全部部长并未排除这种可能性。

paragraphs.speaker: Markwayne Mullins
paragraphs.en: Why would ICE be there? Because only people who should be voting there is American citizens. There shouldn't be any immigration enforcement.
paragraphs.cn: 为什么ICE会在那里？因为只有应该在那里投票的人是美国公民。不应该有任何移民执法。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It was important for President Trump to have a deal with Iran before traveling to Europe for a summit with G-7 leaders. That's what sources are telling CNN. The timing is also crucial, because it came on Trump's 80th birthday and his White House UFC fight to celebrate America's 250th anniversary.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普总统在前往欧洲参加G7峰会之前与伊朗达成协议非常重要。这是消息人士告诉CNN的。时机也很关键，因为这一天恰逢特朗普80岁生日，以及他为庆祝美国250周年而举办的白宫UFC比赛。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, we're bringing in the group chat to talk more: Sara Fischer, CNN senior media analyst and senior media reporter at Axios; Elena Schneider, national political reporter, "NOTUS"; and Francesca Chambers, White House correspondent at "USA Today." So, to set this up, I want to play the message the administration was sending, not to Iran, not to Israel, but to the American people. Here is J.D. Vance, talking on "FOX News Sunday" in the aftermath of the announcement of this memorandum.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我们邀请群聊来进一步讨论：CNN高级媒体分析师兼Axios高级媒体记者Sara Fischer；《NOTUS》全国政治记者Elena Schneider；以及《今日美国》白宫记者Francesca Chambers。为了铺垫，我想播放政府传达的信息，不是给伊朗，不是给以色列，而是给美国人民。以下是J.D. Vance在宣布这份备忘录后在《福克斯周日新闻》上的发言。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: VANCE (via phone): My primary message to the American people is, thank you. Because of your patience, I think that we've solved the problem that has plagued this country again for -- for well before I was even born. But this is just a great thing for the American people. I know that they suffered from high gas prices. The president has certainly been very concerned about that fact. But what we're going to be able to do is drive down the cost of energy, not just now, but for the long term.
paragraphs.cn: 万斯（通过电话）：我给美国人民的主要信息是，谢谢。因为你们的耐心，我认为我们已经解决了这个在我出生之前就困扰这个国家的问题。但这对于美国人民来说是一件大好事。我知道他们承受了高油价。总统当然非常关心这个事实。但我们将能够降低能源成本，不仅是现在，而且是长期。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Francesca, this seems like the -- specifically, this kind of sentence is the message they had hoped to deliver on this day, of so many things, when the president wanted good news. FRANCESCA CHAMBERS, WHITE HOUSE CORRESPONDENT, "USA TODAY": Well, and there's still a long way to go until we see a final deal. They want that to come in the next 60 days.
paragraphs.cn: 弗朗西斯卡，这似乎是 -- -- 具体来说，这种句子是他们希望在这一天传达的信息，在总统想要好消息的众多事情中。《今日美国》白宫记者弗朗西斯卡·钱伯斯：嗯，距离我们看到最终协议还有很长的路要走。他们希望在接下来的60天内达成。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But the experts, critics, even supporters of the administration who have come out, you know, in the last 24 hours are saying that the devil is, essentially, in the details. I mean, Iran may have agreed to give the United States the enriched uranium or down blend it. We still don't know what that looks like. We still don't know whether or not they're going to dismantle their nuclear program. The United States says that they will. But again, that all has to be worked out as part of this final agreement.
paragraphs.cn: 但专家、批评者，甚至政府支持者在过去24小时内都表示，细节决定成败。我的意思是，伊朗可能已经同意向美国提供浓缩铀或将其稀释。我们仍然不知道具体情况。我们仍然不知道他们是否会拆除核计划。美国表示他们会。但同样，这一切都必须在最终协议中解决。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: This is just a memorandum of understanding.
paragraphs.cn: 这只是一份谅解备忘录。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And yet, Vance comes out and is, like, thanks for being patient. It's been a tough couple of weeks.
paragraphs.cn: 然而，万斯出来说，谢谢你们的耐心。这真是艰难的几周。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Let's wrap it up. You can feel the hands going like this. (BRUSHES PALMS TOGETHER) Given all the question marks Francesca just raised, what's the danger of telling everyone this? ELENA SCHNEIDER, NATIONAL POLITICAL REPORTER, "NOTUS": Well, look, I think that there is a political reality that they are reacting to, which is the pain that voters are feeling every single day when they go fill up their cars, and are looking at high gas prices, and connect that to the ongoing operations and, you know, what's -- what's
paragraphs.cn: 让我们总结一下。你可以感觉到手在这样动。（搓手）考虑到弗朗西斯卡刚才提出的所有问号，告诉所有人这些有什么危险？《NOTUS》全国政治记者埃琳娜·施奈德：嗯，我认为他们正在应对一个政治现实，那就是选民每天去加油时感受到的痛苦，看着高油价，并将其与正在进行的行动以及伊朗发生的事情联系起来。他们想尽快庆祝胜利，指出，好吧，这将会改变。我们可以看到这将会改变。他们面临的挑战是，它真的会改变吗？因为即使他们能够在这里达成某种协议；停火得以维持；他们开始就此进行谈判，获得这些供应链，让石油通过霍尔木兹海峡，这些都不会立即发生。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: been happening in Iran. And they want to take a victory lap as soon as possible to point out, say, OK, this is now going to change. We can -- we can see that this is going to change. The challenge for them is, is it actually going to change? Because even if they are able to work out some sort of deal here; the ceasefire holds; they start negotiating on this, getting those supply chains, getting that gas to, you know, through the Strait of Hormuz is not going to happen right away.
paragraphs.cn: 一直在伊朗发生的事情。他们想尽快庆祝胜利，指出，好吧，这将会改变。我们可以看到这将会改变。他们面临的挑战是，它真的会改变吗？因为即使他们能够在这里达成某种协议；停火得以维持；他们开始就此进行谈判，获得这些供应链，让石油通过霍尔木兹海峡，这些都不会立即发生。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And experts have said time and again that -- that -- that gas prices are going to take a little while to come back.
paragraphs.cn: 专家们一再表示，汽油价格需要一段时间才能回落。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. People are looking right now, as you're speaking, at the journey that gas prices will have to take from that peak.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。就在你说话的时候，人们正在关注汽油价格从峰值回落的历程。

paragraphs.speaker: Schneider
paragraphs.en: And it's a long climb back down that peak.
paragraphs.cn: 从那个峰值回落是一个漫长的过程。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And it's -- it's a long climb. The thing is, also, once you say something like that, you kind of start a clock. Like, oh, OK, things are going to get better? When? And when you're looking at disapproval ratings that have moved significantly, when you're looking at questions of specifically how have you handled the economy, for example. And seeing independents, 82 percent disapprove. It feels as though it's a bit of a risk to keep saying no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, it's happening, it's happening.
paragraphs.cn: 而且这是一个漫长的过程。问题是，一旦你说了这样的话，你就等于开始了一个倒计时。比如，哦，好吧，情况会好转？什么时候？当你看到不支持率大幅上升，当你看到诸如你如何处理经济等具体问题时，看到82%的独立选民表示不赞成。继续不停地说"不，不，不，不，不，不，不，不，不，情况正在好转，正在好转"似乎有点冒险。

paragraphs.speaker: Sara Fischer
paragraphs.en: Yes. You have a five-month time clock until the midterms. So, that's the horizon that they're working with. And I think most experts will argue that the supply chain issues are interesting, because there's a lag time between, obviously, when ships can get there or not.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。距离中期选举还有五个月的时间。所以，这是他们需要应对的时间范围。我认为大多数专家会认为供应链问题很有趣，因为显然，船舶能否到达那里存在一个滞后时间。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But can you speak it into existence? Like, if you just say it's -- it's getting better, will it get better?
paragraphs.cn: 但你能通过说出来让它成真吗？比如，如果你只是说情况正在好转，它就会好转吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Fischer
paragraphs.en: You cannot. You cannot. But so, let's say after the 60 days the strait does open. Then you have three months. And that should be enough time, actually, for us to start seeing an impact on oil. And we did see overnight that some of the crude oil prices have gone down on the index. But if there is any delay at that, there's not a lot of wiggle room. And so that's where they run into some problems.
paragraphs.cn: 你不能。你不能。但是，假设60天后海峡确实开放了。那么你还有三个月的时间。实际上，这应该足够让我们开始看到对石油的影响。我们确实在隔夜看到一些原油价格指数下跌了。但如果出现任何延误，就没有多少回旋余地了。这就是他们遇到一些问题的地方。

paragraphs.speaker: Fischer
paragraphs.en: If oil prices are still, in some places, above five or anywhere in the top, you know, top half of the $4 range, you're going to have a lot of problems leading up to the midterms.
paragraphs.cn: 如果油价在某些地方仍然高于5美元，或者处于4美元区间的上半部分，那么在中期选举之前你会遇到很多问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Trump has been so bummed about the way Europe has kind of stayed out of this whole thing. He's going to be meeting with G-7 leaders when they made their various announcements today. I could read them all, but they basically all say they welcome the agreement over there. It seems like they agreed on what they would be saying.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普对欧洲基本上置身事外的态度感到非常沮丧。他将在七国集团领导人今天发表各种声明时与他们见面。我可以把它们都读出来，但它们基本上都表示欢迎那边的协议。看起来他们对自己要说的话达成了一致。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, Francesca, just heading into today, what is the goal for Trump, who's been kind of dismissive of this group of people? Is he going to say to them, you guys weren't helpful, but it would be helpful if you'd get some ships through.
paragraphs.cn: 那么，弗朗西斯卡，就在今天之前，特朗普的目标是什么？他一直对这群人有些不屑一顾。他会对他们说，你们没有帮忙，但如果你们能让一些船只通过，那会有帮助吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Chambers
paragraphs.en: I mean, they welcome their help with demining efforts that would help get the Strait of Hormuz fully reopen. But I did want to just touch on one other thing that you were saying about the vice president for a minute, because he was called by Lindsey Graham, the architect of this agreement, and that was really --
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，他们欢迎在排雷行动上提供帮助，这将有助于霍尔木兹海峡全面重新开放。但我想稍微提一下你刚才说的关于副总统的另一件事，因为林赛·格雷厄姆 -- -- 这项协议的缔造者 -- -- 给他打了电话，那确实 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: He must have been surprised to hear.
paragraphs.cn: 他听到后一定很惊讶。

paragraphs.speaker: Chambers
paragraphs.en: Well, that was -- but it was a very interesting comment, because he was really put -- Lindsey Graham, close ally of the administration, really putting this at the vice president's feet at this point. And so, it's not just the comments that he's making publicly, the vice president. It's these other comments coming from allies of the Trump administration putting this, if it doesn't work out, at the vice president's feet.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，那确实 -- -- 但这是一个非常有趣的评论，因为他真的被 -- -- 林赛·格雷厄姆，政府的亲密盟友，真的把这件事归咎于副总统。所以，不仅仅是副总统公开发表的言论，还有来自特朗普政府盟友的这些言论，如果事情不顺利，就把责任推到副总统身上。

paragraphs.speaker: Chambers
paragraphs.en: Last week, I actually talked to the vice president about the Iraq War, and he told me that he doesn't think it's going to become a quagmire, that we're not even going to be talking about this a year from now. So, the one that -- the statement that you laid out isn't the only mile marker that he's putting forward here.
paragraphs.cn: 上周，我实际上和副总统谈过伊拉克战争，他告诉我他认为这不会变成泥潭，一年后我们甚至不会再谈论这件事。所以，你提出的那个声明并不是他提出的唯一里程碑。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. But can I translate your statement? Lindsey Graham, the hawk, who's been cheering this on the whole time, is trying to put the actual deal at the feet of J.D. Vance, who's not been cheering this on the whole time, so that if it goes sideways, everyone can say somehow Vance was behind a bad deal?
paragraphs.cn: 是的。但我能翻译一下你的话吗？林赛·格雷厄姆，这个一直为此欢呼的鹰派人物，正试图把实际协议的责任推给J.D.万斯，而万斯并没有一直为此欢呼，所以如果事情出了岔子，每个人都可以说万斯在某种程度上支持了一个糟糕的协议？

paragraphs.speaker: Chambers
paragraphs.en: Well, I'm saying that Lindsey Graham is -- in calling him the architect of this negotiation, this deal that's coming forward -- and the vice president was very, very involved -- is putting the onus on him. If this doesn't work out. I'm not --
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我是说林赛·格雷厄姆 -- -- 在称他为这次谈判、即将达成的协议的缔造者时 -- -- 而副总统非常非常深入地参与其中 -- -- 是把责任推给了他。如果事情不顺利的话。我不是 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, yes. So, if it goes bad, everyone can be like, It was Vance.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，是的。所以，如果事情变糟，每个人都可以说，是万斯的错。

paragraphs.speaker: Chambers
paragraphs.en: And he's saying that time will tell. I'm saying that politically, it becomes a risk for the vice president --
paragraphs.cn: 而他说时间会证明一切。我是说从政治角度看，这对副总统来说变成了一个风险 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Chambers
paragraphs.en: -- at that point, when you have allies of the administration saying he was the architect.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 在那个时候，当政府的盟友说他是缔造者时。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Interesting. OK. We're going to talk more about this as we learn more about this memorandum this week. And then after the break on CNN THIS MORNING, did you know that the U.K. is the latest country to ban kids under the age of 16 from all social media? We'll find out whether these bans actually work. Plus --
paragraphs.cn: 有意思。好的。本周随着我们了解更多关于这份备忘录的内容，我们将进一步讨论这个问题。然后在CNN今晨节目的休息之后，你知道吗，英国是最新一个禁止16岁以下儿童使用所有社交媒体的国家？我们将看看这些禁令是否真的有效。另外 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Dana White
paragraphs.en: I can't afford it. There's no (EXPLETIVE DELETED) way we can do this again.
paragraphs.cn: 我负担不起。我们绝对不能再这么干了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The head of the UFC, Dana White, says he won't be doing the White House fight night again, despite it being what he called an overwhelming success. In the meantime, good morning, very early morning to Seattle, which is hosting a World Cup match today. Belgium and Egypt face off at 3 p.m. Eastern.
paragraphs.cn: UFC总裁达纳·怀特表示，尽管他认为白宫格斗之夜取得了巨大成功，但他不会再举办这样的活动。与此同时，早上好，非常早的早晨问候西雅图，今天这里将举办一场世界杯比赛。比利时和埃及将于东部时间下午3点交锋。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: For the people who said there shouldn't be a cage fight at the White House, what do you say?
paragraphs.cn: 对于那些说白宫不应该举办笼斗比赛的人，你们怎么看？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: They might be right, but since it's here, we're going to be here.
paragraphs.cn: 他们可能是对的，但既然已经在这里了，我们就会待在这里。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: I don't agree with everything Donald Trump has -- has been doing this term, but I'm here, and I'm enjoying the moment.
paragraphs.cn: 我并非同意唐纳德·特朗普这一任期所做的所有事情，但我在这里，享受这一刻。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, UFC fight on the White House lawn. Whether you agreed with it or not, it happened. It was real. Weather slightly delayed the start as President Trump took his seat ringside alongside UFC CEO Dana White. Officially, the event was held on Flag Day, which just also happened to be Donald Trump's 80th birthday. And at one point, the crowd sang "Happy Birthday." And after several fights, the night wrapped up just after 1 a.m. With President Trump greeting the new champion in the Octagon.
paragraphs.cn: 好吧，UFC格斗在白宫草坪上举行。无论你同意与否，它确实发生了。这是真实的。天气稍微推迟了开始时间，特朗普总统与UFC首席执行官达纳·怀特一起坐在拳击台旁。官方上，该活动是在国旗日举行的，而这恰好也是唐纳德·特朗普80岁生日。一度，人群唱起了《生日快乐》。经过几场格斗后，活动在凌晨1点后结束，特朗普总统在八角笼中迎接了新冠军。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The event was the first of its kind on the White House grounds. So, we're going to ask, how is it going to be remembered, both for the sport and in U.S. history? Joining me now is Mike Leon. He's the host of the podcast, "Can We Please Talk?" Good morning, Mike. How are you? MIKE LEON, HOST, "CAN WE PLEASE TALK?": Good to see you, Audie. How's everything?
paragraphs.cn: 这是白宫场地上首次举办此类活动。那么，我们要问，它将如何被铭记，无论是对于这项运动还是在美国历史上？现在加入我的是迈克·莱昂。他是播客《我们能谈谈吗？》的主持人。早上好，迈克。你好吗？迈克·莱昂，播客《我们能谈谈吗？》主持人：很高兴见到你，奥黛。一切都好吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, as a fight, was it good? Like, is the card, the tournament? I know the Polymarket, Kalshi prediction markets did very well with all of these different bouts. But what was it like just as a fan?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，作为一场格斗，它精彩吗？比如，比赛卡、锦标赛？我知道Polymarket、Kalshi预测市场在这些不同的比赛中表现非常好。但仅仅作为粉丝，感觉如何？

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: Yes, just as a fan, you know, working in the combat sports space and overseeing productions. From a production standpoint, it really was immersive, expansive. I mean, we could talk about you couldn't watch it unless you had a Paramount+ subscription. It wasn't free over the air like on CBS, which I thought the president probably would have done, or at least Dana White would have done.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，仅仅作为粉丝，你知道，我在格斗运动领域工作并负责制作。从制作角度来看，它确实沉浸式、规模宏大。我的意思是，我们可以说，除非你有Paramount+订阅，否则你看不到。它不是像CBS那样免费播出的，我以为总统可能会这样做，或者至少达纳·怀特会这样做。

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: But you've got to recoup those costs of the 60 million that they spent to -- to do all of this and the Claw and everything that they implemented. But just from a TV perspective, watching it, the show, the programing that they did. Chris Weidman is a friend of mine who happened to do some of the pre-fight coverage there. It really was a true event outside of a typical arena or some type of setting that houses combat sports. But there's a bunch of things underlying, in terms of the event with respect to politics.
paragraphs.cn: 但你必须收回他们花费的6000万美元成本，以及他们实施的"爪子"等一切。但仅从电视角度来看，观看它，他们所做的节目和编排。克里斯·韦德曼是我的朋友，他碰巧在那里做了一些赛前报道。这确实是一个超越典型竞技场或格斗运动场所的真正赛事。但在政治方面，这个活动背后有很多潜在因素。

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: But from a fight night perspective, Audie, it actually was a pretty decent sporting event.
paragraphs.cn: 但从比赛之夜的角度来看，Audie，这实际上是一场相当不错的体育赛事。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And to your point, Dana White pretty much was, like, I'm not going to do this again just because of the sheer expense.
paragraphs.cn: 正如你所说，Dana White 基本上表示，仅仅因为巨大的开销，他不会再来一次了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But I want to get to something which is who was, you know, in the Octagon and who was not. You had UFC middleweight champion Sean Strickland. He was off the card. And he's the reigning champ, right, middleweight champ. And Strickland thinks he knows why. He basically has turned down -- he's been this outspoken, you know, critic of Trump on social media.
paragraphs.cn: 但我想谈谈谁在八角笼里，谁不在。UFC 中量级冠军 Sean Strickland 不在比赛名单上。他是现任冠军，对吧，中量级冠军。Strickland 认为他知道原因。他基本上拒绝了 -- -- 他在社交媒体上一直是特朗普直言不讳的批评者。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: He says that he stopped supporting Trump after, quote, "Israel made him bomb Iran the first time." Another thing he's posted, saying that he got a call from UFC saying he, quote, "wasn't cleared" by the White House. And honestly, he showed up anyway; in an extreme MMA on-brand situation was escorted out by park police. I mean, it actually became like a bit of a thing here. Help me make sense of this. It's a big UFC night. A big UFC star's getting hauled out with all this security. What's the politics?
paragraphs.cn: 他说他不再支持特朗普，因为"以色列让他第一次轰炸伊朗"。他还发帖说，他接到 UFC 的电话，称白宫认为他"未获批准"。老实说，他还是出现了；在极端 MMA 风格的情况下，他被公园警察护送出去。我的意思是，这实际上成了个事儿。帮我理解一下。这是一个重要的 UFC 之夜。一个 UFC 大明星被这么多安保人员拖走。政治因素是什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: I mean, remember, there's an element of how UFC is also in partnership with the WWE, right? There's a performative element with some of that, for Strickland doing that. And there's also guys who fought on the card. I don't know if you saw the video, Audie, of the -- the person who said the comments about Michelle Obama in the post-fight interview with Joe Rogan, which was disgusting. There are some things that are, from a performance standpoint, always going to mirror what we kind of see in WWE.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，记住，UFC 也与 WWE 有合作，对吧？Strickland 这么做有表演成分。还有一些参加比赛的人。我不知道你是否看到了视频，Audie，那个在赛后采访中对 Joe Rogan 说关于 Michelle Obama 的恶心话的人。从表演角度来看，有些东西总是会反映我们在 WWE 中看到的东西。

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: Strickland is right, to a certain extent. There's a reason why he was left off the card. Non-politics perspective wise. There were a few other fights that actually dropped out from this card. But in terms of the event overall, like I said, it was a pretty solid event. But to make sense of it, Audie, I mean, they get punched in the head; they grapple. Sometimes people say different things, and they have different political leanings. This is what you get in the combat sports space.
paragraphs.cn: Strickland 在某种程度上是对的。他不在比赛名单上是有原因的。从非政治角度来看，还有其他几场比赛实际上退出了。但就整个赛事而言，就像我说的，这是一场相当扎实的赛事。但要理解它，Audie，我的意思是，他们被击中头部；他们扭打。有时人们说不同的话，有不同的政治倾向。这就是你在格斗运动中得到的。

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: I remember working an event where one of our fighters, before the election in '24, wore Trump trunks. And I remember saying to him in the locker room, "Could you not?" Like, again, freedom of speech. You can wear whatever you want, but like, you're going to get more attention doing this. And he's, like, I'm going to do it anyway. And then he went out there and did it anyway. So, that's -- if you want me to make sense of it, Audie, that's the way to make sense of it. There is no sense.
paragraphs.cn: 我记得在一次活动中，我们的一名选手在 2024 年大选前穿着特朗普短裤。我记得在更衣室里对他说："你能不能别这样？" 同样，言论自由。你可以穿任何你想穿的，但你会因此得到更多关注。他说，我还是要穿。然后他出去还是穿了。所以，如果你想让我解释，Audie，这就是解释。没有道理可言。

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: They get punched in the head for a living.
paragraphs.cn: 他们靠被击中头部谋生。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Does this win over some of the young men that Trump is losing in some of his approval ratings?
paragraphs.cn: 这能赢得一些特朗普在支持率上失去的年轻男性吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: Audie, you and I talked about this last week when I was in studio with you. Trump has always used the combat sports space as this outlet and vehicle to talk to voters, whether it be his stuff that he did in the boxing space years ago, his relationship with Dana White, obviously with the Ellison family, with Paramount. He has always been in the combat sports space, because he thinks it makes him seem relatable, that he loves these sports. And these are the type of fans.
paragraphs.cn: Audie，上周我在演播室和你讨论过这个。特朗普一直利用格斗运动作为与选民交流的出口和工具，无论是他多年前在拳击领域做的事情，他与 Dana White 的关系，显然还有与 Ellison 家族和 Paramount 的关系。他一直活跃在格斗运动领域，因为他认为这让他显得平易近人，他热爱这些运动。而这些正是这类粉丝。

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: And again, speaking with conservative voters over years in different cities, they've talked about his attendance at these events. And Democrats don't go to these type of things, whereas Trump does. I told you last week, Ruben Gallego and I've had this conversation about Democrats potentially leaning into, like, boxing events, to the relationships that I have in the combat sports space. And it's just -- there's voters there that I feel are being left out. And they feel they're being left out of the conversation.
paragraphs.cn: 再说一次，多年来在不同城市与保守派选民交谈时，他们提到了他出席这些活动的情况。民主党人不参加这类活动，而特朗普会去。上周我告诉过你，鲁本·加列戈和我讨论过民主党可能倾向于参与拳击赛事，以及我在格斗运动领域的人脉。只是 -- -- 那里有一些选民，我觉得他们被忽视了。而他们自己也觉得被排除在对话之外。

paragraphs.speaker: Leon

vocabulary.cn: 霍尔木兹海峡（连接波斯湾与印度洋的战略水道）
vocabulary.en: a narrow strait between the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman, crucial for global oil shipments
vocabulary.excerpt: both the U.S. and Iran say there's an agreement to open the Strait of Hormuz on Friday.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 美国和伊朗都表示已达成协议，将于周五开放霍尔木兹海峡。

vocabulary.cn: 谈判者；协商者
vocabulary.en: a person who conducts negotiations
vocabulary.excerpt: The Iranians are incredibly difficult negotiators.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 伊朗人是极其难缠的谈判者。

vocabulary.cn: 酸葡萄心理（因得不到而贬低）
vocabulary.en: an expression meaning to disparage something one cannot have
vocabulary.excerpt: True or just sour grapes?
vocabulary.exampleCn: 这是真的还是酸葡萄心理？

vocabulary.cn: 跳伞者；跳伞运动员
vocabulary.en: a person who performs skydiving
vocabulary.excerpt: A plane full of skydivers crashes right after takeoff.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 一架满载跳伞者的飞机起飞后立即坠毁。

vocabulary.cn: 住院的；被送入医院的
vocabulary.en: admitted to a hospital for treatment
vocabulary.excerpt: Senator Mitch McConnell hospitalized.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 参议员米奇·麦康奈尔住院。

vocabulary.cn: 社交媒体禁令
vocabulary.en: a prohibition on the use of social media platforms
vocabulary.excerpt: But will a new social media ban actually work?
vocabulary.exampleCn: 但新的社交媒体禁令真的会奏效吗？

vocabulary.cn: 终极格斗冠军赛（Ultimate Fighting Championship）
vocabulary.en: a mixed martial arts promotion company
vocabulary.excerpt: UFC fight night at the White House lawn.
vocabulary.exampleCn: UFC格斗之夜在白宫草坪举行。

vocabulary.cn: 斯坦利杯（NHL冠军奖杯）
vocabulary.en: the championship trophy awarded annually to the National Hockey League (NHL) playoff winner
vocabulary.excerpt: the Carolina Hurricanes are Stanley Cup champions.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 卡罗来纳飓风队是斯坦利杯冠军。

vocabulary.cn: 完封（体育比赛中一方未得分）
vocabulary.en: a game in which the opposing team fails to score
vocabulary.excerpt: capping off the season with a shutout over the Las Vegas Golden Knights.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 以完封拉斯维加斯金骑士队结束赛季。

vocabulary.cn: 外交突破
vocabulary.en: a significant advance in diplomatic relations
vocabulary.excerpt: A diplomatic breakthrough: The U.S. and Iran say they have reached a deal.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 外交突破：美国和伊朗称已达成协议。

vocabulary.cn: 停火；停战
vocabulary.en: a temporary suspension of fighting
vocabulary.excerpt: Sometimes these ceasefires take a little bit of time to take root.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 有时这些停火需要一点时间才能扎根。

vocabulary.cn: （讽刺）和谐一致；唱赞歌（源自灵歌，常指不切实际的乐观）
vocabulary.en: used ironically to refer to a naive or overly optimistic view of harmony
vocabulary.excerpt: I'm not going to say that everybody is going to sing kumbaya tomorrow.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我不会说明天大家就会唱起和谐赞歌。

vocabulary.cn: 谅解备忘录（MOU，非正式协议）
vocabulary.en: a non-binding agreement outlining the terms of a deal
vocabulary.excerpt: This memorandum of understanding was reached, despite our complete distrust of our enemies.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 尽管我们完全不相信敌人，但这份谅解备忘录还是达成了。

vocabulary.cn: 实施；执行
vocabulary.en: the process of putting a decision or plan into effect
vocabulary.excerpt: We have specific plans in place to monitor the implementation of U.S. commitments.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我们有具体计划来监督美国承诺的执行。

vocabulary.cn: 使偏离轨道；破坏（计划、进程）
vocabulary.en: to obstruct or prevent (a process) from continuing
vocabulary.excerpt: So, could this derail things?
vocabulary.exampleCn: 那么，这会不会破坏事情？

vocabulary.cn: 强硬的；不妥协的
vocabulary.en: uncompromising; strict
vocabulary.excerpt: this regime, in particular, is more hardline than the previous regime.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 这个政权尤其比之前的政权更加强硬。

vocabulary.cn: 棘手的；麻烦的
vocabulary.en: causing difficulty; problematic
vocabulary.excerpt: It does not address the thorniest issues.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 它没有解决最棘手的问题。

vocabulary.cn: 高浓缩铀（可用于核武器）
vocabulary.en: uranium with a high concentration of the isotope U-235, suitable for nuclear weapons
vocabulary.excerpt: the highly enriched uranium that is still sitting in Iran.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 仍存在于伊朗的高浓缩铀。

vocabulary.cn: 军事行动（委婉语，指动武）
vocabulary.en: military action involving the use of force
vocabulary.excerpt: the United States is not going to want to start up kinetic action right before the midterms.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 美国不希望在中期选举前发起军事行动。

vocabulary.cn: 中期选举（美国国会选举）
vocabulary.en: elections held in the middle of a presidential term
vocabulary.excerpt: right before the midterms.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 就在中期选举之前。

vocabulary.cn: 交叉火力；交火
vocabulary.en: gunfire from two or more directions
vocabulary.excerpt: if a drone is launched from Iran or there's crossfire between the two sides.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 如果伊朗发射无人机或双方发生交火。

vocabulary.cn: 代理人；代理（如代理人战争）
vocabulary.en: a person or entity authorized to act on behalf of another
vocabulary.excerpt: proxies like Hezbollah.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 像真主党这样的代理人。

vocabulary.cn: 弹道导弹
vocabulary.en: a missile that follows a ballistic trajectory
vocabulary.excerpt: It doesn't even talk about ballistic missiles.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 它甚至没有提到弹道导弹。

vocabulary.cn: 《联合全面行动计划》（伊朗核协议）
vocabulary.en: Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action, the 2015 nuclear deal with Iran
vocabulary.excerpt: you might not have agreed with the JCPOA.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 你可能不同意《联合全面行动计划》。

sentences.en: Today in the group chat, both the U.S. and Iran say there's an agreement to open the Strait of Hormuz on Friday.
sentences.cn: 今天在群聊中，美国和伊朗都表示已达成协议，将于周五开放霍尔木兹海峡。
sentences.structure: 主句（both the U.S. and Iran say）+ 宾语从句（there's an agreement to open the Strait of Hormuz on Friday），句首为时间状语。
sentences.analysis: 新闻压缩表达：'group chat' 指节目中的讨论环节；'there's an agreement to open' 为 there be 结构，不定式表目的。

sentences.en: A plane full of skydivers crashes right after takeoff, killing everyone on board.
sentences.cn: 一架满载跳伞者的飞机在起飞后立即坠毁，机上人员全部遇难。
sentences.structure: 主句（A plane full of skydivers crashes right after takeoff）+ 伴随状语（killing everyone on board），现在分词短语表结果。
sentences.analysis: 分词短语作结果状语：'killing everyone on board' 补充说明坠机后果；'full of skydivers' 为形容词短语后置定语。

sentences.en: In the U.K., they want to give their kids their childhood back.
sentences.cn: 在英国，他们希望把童年还给孩子。
sentences.structure: 地点状语（In the U.K.）+ 主句（they want to give their kids their childhood back），双宾语结构。
sentences.analysis: 双宾语：'give their kids their childhood back' 中 'their kids' 为间接宾语，'their childhood back' 为直接宾语；'back' 为副词，表示归还。

sentences.en: A diplomatic breakthrough: The U.S. and Iran say they have reached a deal to end the war and to reopen the strait of Hormuz.
sentences.cn: 外交突破：美国和伊朗表示已达成协议，结束战争并重新开放霍尔木兹海峡。
sentences.structure: 同位语结构（A diplomatic breakthrough）+ 主句（The U.S. and Iran say）+ 宾语从句（they have reached a deal to end the war and to reopen the strait of Hormuz），不定式并列作定语。
sentences.analysis: 不定式并列作后置定语：'to end the war and to reopen the strait of Hormuz' 修饰 'deal'；冒号引出具体内容。

sentences.en: Sometimes these ceasefires take a little bit of time to take root.
sentences.cn: 有时这些停火协议需要一点时间才能扎根。
sentences.structure: 主句（these ceasefires take a little bit of time）+ 不定式状语（to take root）。
sentences.analysis: 固定搭配：'take time to do sth.' 意为“花时间做某事”；'take root' 比喻“扎根、生效”。

sentences.en: I'm not going to say that everybody is going to sing kumbaya tomorrow.
sentences.cn: 我不会说明天大家就会唱起《Kumbaya》。
sentences.structure: 主句（I'm not going to say）+ 宾语从句（that everybody is going to sing kumbaya tomorrow）。
sentences.analysis: 文化引用：'sing kumbaya' 源自民谣，常用来讽刺不切实际的和谐或团结；'be going to' 表将来。

sentences.en: But I do think we took a major, major step.
sentences.cn: 但我确实认为我们迈出了非常重大的一步。
sentences.structure: 主句（I do think）+ 宾语从句（we took a major, major step），'do' 为强调助动词。
sentences.analysis: 强调结构：'do' 用于强调谓语动词 'think'；'major, major' 重复表程度加深。

sentences.en: Tehran saying that 60 days of nuclear negotiations will begin once the U.S. meets three commitments.
sentences.cn: 德黑兰表示，一旦美国满足三项承诺，为期60天的核谈判将开始。
sentences.structure: 独立主格结构（Tehran saying）+ 宾语从句（that 60 days of nuclear negotiations will begin once the U.S. meets three commitments），'once' 引导时间状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 独立主格：'Tehran saying' 为现在分词独立结构，相当于 'Tehran says'；'once' 引导条件状语从句，意为“一旦”。

sentences.en: This memorandum of understanding was reached, despite our complete distrust of our enemies.
sentences.cn: 这份谅解备忘录已经达成，尽管我们完全不相信我们的敌人。
sentences.structure: 主句（This memorandum of understanding was reached）+ 让步状语（despite our complete distrust of our enemies）。
sentences.analysis: 介词短语表让步：'despite' 后接名词短语；'distrust of' 为固定搭配，意为“对……的不信任”。

sentences.en: Our trust lies in our military strength, our diplomatic capabilities, and the support and unity of our own people.
sentences.cn: 我们的信任在于我们的军事实力、外交能力以及本国人民的支持与团结。
sentences.structure: 主句（Our trust lies in）+ 并列宾语（our military strength, our diplomatic capabilities, and the support and unity of our own people）。
sentences.analysis: 并列结构：三个名词短语并列作 'lies in' 的宾语；'lie in' 意为“在于”。

sentences.en: That is why the implementation of this memorandum of understanding is extremely important.
sentences.cn: 这就是为什么这份谅解备忘录的执行极其重要。
sentences.structure: 主句（That is）+ 表语从句（why the implementation of this memorandum of understanding is extremely important）。
sentences.analysis: 表语从句：'why' 引导的从句作 'is' 的表语，说明原因；'implementation of' 为名词短语。

sentences.en: But a U.S. official is saying that no funds will be released before Iran implements its commitments.
sentences.cn: 但一位美国官员表示，在伊朗履行其承诺之前，不会释放任何资金。
sentences.structure: 主句（a U.S. official is saying）+ 宾语从句（that no funds will be released before Iran implements its commitments），'before' 引导时间状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 时间状语从句：'before' 引导的从句表示“在……之前”；'release funds' 意为“释放资金”。

sentences.en: The Iranians are incredibly difficult negotiators, coupled with the fact that they're having a very hard time getting guidance from their supreme leader.
sentences.cn: 伊朗人是极其难缠的谈判者，再加上他们很难从最高领袖那里获得指导。
sentences.structure: 主句（The Iranians are incredibly difficult negotiators）+ 伴随状语（coupled with the fact that...），'that' 引导同位语从句。
sentences.analysis: 过去分词短语作伴随状语：'coupled with' 意为“加上”；同位语从句 'that they're having a very hard time getting guidance' 解释 'the fact'。

sentences.en: And they're not always on the same page within their team, between the civilians and military.
sentences.cn: 而且他们团队内部、文职与军方之间并不总是意见一致。
sentences.structure: 主句（they're not always on the same page）+ 地点状语（within their team, between the civilians and military）。
sentences.analysis: 固定短语：'on the same page' 意为“意见一致”；'between...and...' 表示范围。

sentences.en: I think what's interesting about this memorandum of understanding, or this new agreement, is it's only there to open the straits.
sentences.cn: 我认为这份谅解备忘录或这项新协议的有趣之处在于，它只是为了开放海峡。
sentences.structure: 主句（I think）+ 宾语从句（what's interesting about this memorandum of understanding, or this new agreement, is it's only there to open the straits），其中 'what' 引导主语从句，'is' 后为表语从句。
sentences.analysis: 主语从句：'what's interesting...' 作主语；'or this new agreement' 为插入语，解释 'memorandum of understanding'；'only there to' 表目的。

sentences.en: It does not address the thorniest issues, which we know Iran is really going to drag its feet on, which are the nuclear -- its nuclear program and the highly enriched uranium that is still sitting in Iran.
sentences.cn: 它没有解决最棘手的问题，我们知道伊朗在这些问题上会故意拖延，即其核计划以及仍存放在伊朗的高浓缩铀。
sentences.structure: 主句（It does not address the thorniest issues）+ 非限制性定语从句（which we know Iran is really going to drag its feet on）+ 另一个非限制性定语从句（which are the nuclear...），其中 'that' 引导定语从句修饰 'uranium'。
sentences.analysis: 嵌套定语从句：第一个 'which' 指代 'issues'，第二个 'which' 指代 'thorniest issues'；'drag one's feet on' 意为“故意拖延”；'highly enriched uranium' 为专业术语。

sentences.en: And Iran, like -- like Mike Waltz was saying, Iran knows how to play the game to draw the United States out.
sentences.cn: 而伊朗，就像迈克·沃尔兹所说的，伊朗知道如何玩这场游戏来引诱美国。
sentences.structure: 插入语（like Mike Waltz was saying）+ 主句（Iran knows how to play the game to draw the United States out），'how to' 为疑问词+不定式结构。
sentences.analysis: 插入语：'like Mike Waltz was saying' 表示引用；'play the game' 比喻“玩弄策略”；'draw...out' 意为“引出、诱使”。

sentences.en: If we're talking about negotiating, at minimum, within 60 days, that is bringing us up right up to the midterms.
sentences.cn: 如果我们谈论的是谈判，至少60天内，那就会把我们直接带到中期选举。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句（If we're talking about negotiating）+ 插入语（at minimum）+ 主句（that is bringing us up right up to the midterms），'within 60 days' 为时间状语。
sentences.analysis: 条件句：'if' 引导真实条件；'at minimum' 为插入语，意为“至少”；'bring up to' 意为“带到”；'midterms' 指中期选举。

sentences.en: And I think Iran knows that the United States is not going to want to start up kinetic action right before the midterms, right before voting starts.
sentences.cn: 而且我认为伊朗知道，美国不会想在中期选举前、投票开始前发动军事行动。
sentences.structure: 主句（I think）+ 宾语从句（Iran knows that...），'that' 引导宾语从句，其中 'right before' 引导时间状语。
sentences.analysis: 多层宾语从句：'Iran knows' 后接 'that' 从句；'kinetic action' 为军事术语，意为“军事行动”；'right before' 强调“就在……之前”。

sentences.en: I think both sides could say today that the strait is open and that they're clearing the mines from the strait.
sentences.cn: 我认为双方今天都可以说海峡是开放的，并且他们正在清除海峡中的水雷。
sentences.structure: 主句（I think）+ 宾语从句（both sides could say today that...and that...），两个 'that' 引导并列的宾语从句。
sentences.analysis: 并列宾语从句：两个 'that' 从句并列作 'say' 的宾语；'clear the mines' 意为“清除水雷”。

topics.title: 美伊霍尔木兹海峡协议
topics.content: 美国与伊朗达成谅解备忘录，计划于2026年6月19日（周五）开放霍尔木兹海峡（Strait of Hormuz）。伊朗提出三项前提条件：解除海军封锁、停止军事行动、解冻数十亿美元资金。美方官员否认立即放款，称伊朗需先履行承诺。协议未涉及核问题（nuclear program）及弹道导弹（ballistic missiles），被视为临时性安排。
topics.keywords: Strait of Hormuz · memorandum of understanding · nuclear program · ballistic missiles

topics.title: 以色列对协议的不满
topics.content: 以色列对美伊协议表示不满，认为协议未解决伊朗支持的代理人武装（proxies）如真主党（Hezbollah）以及弹道导弹问题。以色列未参与谈判，可能继续在黎巴嫩开展军事行动。美国总统特朗普公开批评以色列总理内塔尼亚胡（Bibi），担心以色列的行动破坏协议。
topics.keywords: proxies · Hezbollah · Benjamin Netanyahu · military operations

topics.title: 美国中期选举与伊朗谈判策略
topics.content: 分析人士指出，伊朗利用谈判时间表将关键节点推至美国中期选举（midterms）前夕，使美国不愿在选举前采取军事行动（kinetic action）。伊朗谈判风格强硬（hardline），内部存在文职与军方分歧，但善于拖延和施压。

topics.title: 其他新闻事件
topics.content: 本期节目还报道了：一架载有跳伞者（skydivers）的飞机起飞后坠毁，全员遇难；参议员米奇·麦康奈尔（Mitch McConnell）住院；英国拟推行社交媒体禁令（social media ban）以保护青少年；UFC（终极格斗冠军赛）在白宫草坪举办比赛；卡罗来纳飓风队（Carolina Hurricanes）完封（shutout）拉斯维加斯金骑士队，夺得斯坦利杯（Stanley Cup）。
topics.keywords: skydivers · Mitch McConnell · social media ban · UFC · Stanley Cup · shutout

quiz.question: 原文 'The Iranians are incredibly difficult negotiators.' 中 'negotiators' 的意思是？
options: A. 谈判者
B. 调解者
C. 观察员
D. 翻译
quiz.explanation: negotiator 意为 '谈判者'，指参与协商的人。原文中美国大使形容伊朗人是 'difficult negotiators'（难缠的谈判者）。

quiz.question: 原文 'It does not address the thorniest issues.' 中 'thorniest' 的意思是？
options: A. 最尖锐的
B. 最棘手的
C. 最危险的
D. 最复杂的
quiz.explanation: thorny 本义 '多刺的'，引申为 '棘手的、麻烦的'。thorniest 是最高级，指 '最棘手的'。

quiz.question: 原文 'the United States is not going to want to start up kinetic action right before the midterms.' 中 'kinetic action' 指的是？
options: A. 外交行动
B. 经济制裁
C. 军事行动
D. 法律行动
quiz.explanation: kinetic action 是军事委婉语，意为 '军事行动'，指动用武力。

quiz.question: 根据报道，美伊协议的主要目的是什么？
options: A. 彻底解决伊朗核问题
B. 开放霍尔木兹海峡
C. 解除对伊朗的所有制裁
D. 建立长期和平关系
quiz.explanation: 报道明确指出协议是为了 'open the Strait of Hormuz on Friday'，即开放霍尔木兹海峡。核问题等更棘手议题未被涵盖。

quiz.question: 伊朗提出的三个前提条件不包括以下哪一项？
options: A. 美国结束海军封锁
B. 美国停止军事行动
C. 美国释放冻结的伊朗资金
D. 美国承认伊朗核权利
quiz.explanation: 报道中伊朗要求：结束海军封锁、停止军事行动、释放数十亿美元冻结资金。未提及承认核权利。

quiz.question: 为什么分析人士认为伊朗将谈判拖延至60天后？
options: A. 等待国际社会介入
B. 利用美国中期选举前的政治敏感期
C. 等待新领导人上任
D. 为军事准备争取时间
quiz.explanation: 报道指出，60天谈判期将 'bringing us up right up to the midterms'，伊朗知道美国不愿在选举前发动军事行动，从而获得谈判优势。
